content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(1, 9, 2), (2, 4, 3), (2, 9, 3), (5, 8, 5), (0, 7, 6), (0, 4, 11), (3, 4, 12), (5, 9, 13), (3, 7, 16), (1, 7, 17), (4, 7, 17), (5, 6, 20), (4, 5, 22), (0, 5, 27), (1, 5, 29)]

We need to find the nodes linked with node 5 exactly at time 20.

First, we will scan each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (1, 9, 2) has node 1 and time 2. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (2, 4, 3) has node 2 and time 3. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 9, 3) has node 2 and time 3. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (5, 8, 5) has node 5 and time 5. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (0, 7, 6) has node 0 and time 6. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 4, 11) has node 0 and time 11. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (3, 4, 12) has node 3 and time 12. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (5, 9, 13) has node 5 and time 13. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (3, 7, 16) has node 3 and time 16. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (1, 7, 17) has node 1 and time 17. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (4, 7, 17) has node 4 and time 17. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (5, 6, 20) has node 5 and time 20. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (4, 5, 22) has node 4 and time 22. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (0, 5, 27) has node 0 and time 27. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (1, 5, 29) has node 1 and time 29. -> Add node 5.

Now we have the following nodes linked with node 5 at time 20:
8, 4, 7, 6

Therefore, the answer is: [8, 4, 7, 6]

Answer: [8, 4, 7, 6]
